title: Performance Enhancing Drugs
tagline: Hormone classes, axis pathways, and high-yield agents — taught for medicine, not a how-to guide.
disclaimer: Educational pharmacology and physiology only. Abuse carries serious medical, legal, and sporting consequences.

steroid.label: Steroid hormones
steroid.summary: Lipid-derived, ring-structured hormones. Most act on intracellular nuclear receptors — gene transcription after entering the cell (or are already synthesized inside target cells).
steroid.where: Nuclear hormone receptors in cytoplasm/nucleus of target cells. Classic axes: HPG (gonadal), adrenal (cortisol), mineralocorticoid.

agents.name: Testosterone
agents.where: Androgen receptor (nuclear) — muscle, bone, erythropoiesis, CNS
agents.pedNote: Exogenous forms (enanthate, cypionate) prolong half-life; suppresses HPG via negative feedback.
agents.pearl: Aromatized to estradiol; 5α-reduced to DHT (potent androgen).

agents.name: Dihydrotestosterone (DHT)
agents.where: Androgen receptor — prostate, skin, hair follicles
agents.pedNote: Not a common injectable; finasteride blocks conversion (side effect exploited rarely).
agents.pearl: 5α-reductase converts T → DHT; drives prostate growth and male-pattern hair effects.

agents.name: Estradiol
agents.where: Estrogen receptors (ERα/ERβ) — bone, CV, breast, feedback on HPG
agents.pedNote: Gynecomastia on AAS cycles; tamoxifen/aromatase inhibitors used to mask.
agents.pearl: Aromatase (CYP19) in adipose/Leydig converts androgens → estrogens.

agents.name: Cortisol
agents.where: Glucocorticoid receptor — anti-inflammatory, gluconeogenesis, immunosuppression
agents.pedNote: Low-dose dexamethasone abused to suppress inflammation/mask injury; catabolic at high dose.
agents.pearl: Exogenous glucocorticoids ↓ ACTH/CRH via negative feedback — adrenal suppression risk.

agents.name: Aldosterone
agents.where: Mineralocorticoid receptor — kidney distal tubule Na⁺ retention
agents.pedNote: Indirectly relevant via fluid balance; fludrocortisone rarely abused.
agents.pearl: Spironolactone blocks MR — also anti-androgen (gynecomastia).

agents.name: Nandrolone
agents.where: Androgen receptor — anabolic > androgenic vs testosterone
agents.pedNote: Long metabolite detection window; classic 'Deca' in doping literature.
agents.pearl: 19-nor structure; less DHT but still aromatizes to estrogenic metabolites.

agents.name: Stanozolol
agents.where: Androgen receptor — oral bioavailability via 17α-alkylation
agents.pedNote: Oral 17α-alkylated steroids stress the liver (cholestasis, peliosis).
agents.pearl: 17α-alkyl group prevents first-pass degradation — hepatotoxicity trade-off.

agents.name: Oxandrolone
agents.where: Androgen receptor — relatively anabolic
agents.pedNote: Medically used for wasting; abused for lean mass with lower androgenic rating on paper.
agents.pearl: Still 17α-alkylated — not 'safe' for the liver despite 'mild' reputation.

agents.name: Boldenone
agents.where: Androgen receptor
agents.pedNote: Equipoise; long ester; detected in anti-doping panels.
agents.pearl: Structurally similar to testosterone with a double bond — distinct metabolites for testing.

agents.name: Tamoxifen
agents.where: Estrogen receptor antagonist (breast) / partial agonist elsewhere
agents.pathway: Blocks estradiol at ER — not a steroid hormone itself
agents.pedNote: Masking gynecomastia on AAS cycles; not an anabolic agent.
agents.pearl: SERM: antagonist in breast, agonist in bone/uterus — know tissue selectivity.

agents.name: Anastrozole
agents.where: Inhibits CYP19 — ↓ estradiol synthesis
agents.pedNote: Reduces estrogen during high-testosterone abuse; bone density risk long-term.
agents.pearl: Non-steroidal AI; lowers E2 without blocking ER directly.

peptide.label: Peptide hormones
peptide.summary: Amino-acid chains. Bind cell-surface receptors → second messengers (cAMP, JAK-STAT, tyrosine kinase cascades). Cannot orally absorb intact — injected or inhaled (some).
peptide.where: Plasma membrane receptors on target cells. Often secreted from pituitary, hypothalamus, kidney, pancreas.

agents.name: Growth hormone (GH)
agents.where: GH receptor (JAK2/STAT) — liver, bone, muscle; ↑ IGF-1
agents.pedNote: rhGH abuse; hard to detect (pulsatile, short half-life); lipolysis + anabolic via IGF-1.
agents.pearl: GH is pulsatile; IGF-1 is the stable downstream marker in blood.

agents.name: IGF-1
agents.where: IGF-1 receptor (tyrosine kinase) — muscle, bone
agents.pathway: Liver + local production downstream of GH
agents.pedNote: IGF-1 LR3 and DES variants abused; hypoglycemia risk.
agents.pearl: Insulin-like structure; GH → liver IGF-1 is the main endocrine loop.

agents.name: Erythropoietin (EPO)
agents.where: EPO receptor (JAK2) on erythroid progenitors in bone marrow
agents.pathway: Hypoxia → HIF → renal EPO → ↑ RBC mass
agents.pedNote: rhEPO / darbepoetin ↑ hematocrit → endurance; thrombosis risk.
agents.pearl: Active rhEPO abuse: ↑ hematocrit/Hgb WITH elevated reticulocytes; reticulocytes fall after stopping (OFF-score / Athlete Biological Passport).

agents.name: Insulin
agents.where: Insulin receptor (tyrosine kinase) — muscle, fat, liver
agents.pedNote: Abused with GH/IGF for anabolism; fatal hypoglycemia risk.
agents.pearl: Counter-regulatory: glucagon, epinephrine, cortisol, GH raise glucose.

agents.name: hCG
agents.where: LH/CG receptor on Leydig cells → ↑ testosterone
agents.pedNote: Used to restart/testicular function after AAS; itself detectable.
agents.pearl: α subunit like LH/FSH/TSH; β subunit unique — immunoassays target β-hCG.

agents.name: ACTH
agents.where: MC2 receptor on adrenal cortex → cortisol
agents.pedNote: Rare direct abuse; tetracosactide (ACTH analogue) in doping history.
agents.pearl: ACTH 1–39; shorter fragments used diagnostically (stimulation test).

agents.name: GnRH
agents.where: GnRH receptor on pituitary gonadotrophs → LH/FSH pulse
agents.pedNote: Continuous GnRH agonists paradoxically suppress LH/FSH (downregulation).
agents.pearl: Pulsatile = stimulate; continuous infusion = chemical castration (prostate CA).

agents.name: LH / FSH
agents.where: Gonadal receptors — Leydig (LH), Sertoli/granulosa (FSH)
agents.pedNote: Less common as black-market agents vs hCG or exogenous testosterone.
agents.pearl: LH drives testosterone; FSH drives spermatogenesis / folliculogenesis.

agents.name: GHRP / GHRH analogs
agents.where: GHRH receptor or ghrelin/GH secretagogue receptor → ↑ GH pulse
agents.pedNote: CJC-1295, ipamorelin, etc. — peptide doping category.
agents.pearl: Work by amplifying endogenous GH pulses rather than replacing GH protein.

agents.name: Glucagon
agents.where: Glucagon receptor — hepatocyte glycogenolysis/gluconeogenesis
agents.pedNote: Not performance-enhancing; know for hypoglycemia rescue contrast.
agents.pearl: Raises blood glucose; opposite net effect to insulin on liver.

amine.label: Amine hormones
amine.summary: Derived from tyrosine or tryptophan. Catecholamines and thyroid hormones — faster signaling or iodinated amines acting on nuclear receptors.
amine.where: Catecholamines: GPCRs (α/β adrenergic). Thyroid hormones: nuclear receptors (TR) after cellular uptake.

agents.name: Epinephrine
agents.where: α1, β1, β2 adrenergic receptors — heart, bronchi, vasculature
agents.pedNote: Not a chronic PED; acute performance in theory; endogenous in stress.
agents.pearl: β2 agonism → bronchodilation + muscle tremor; β1 ↑ HR/contractility.

agents.name: Norepinephrine
agents.where: α1 > β1 vasoconstriction + cardiac output
agents.pedNote: Relevance is sympathomimetic drug class overlap.
agents.pearl: Less β2 activity than epinephrine — more vasoconstrictive.

agents.name: Dopamine
agents.where: Dopamine receptors (CNS); precursor to NE/Epi
agents.pedNote: Bromocriptine/cabergoline (dopamine agonists) suppress prolactin on cycles.
agents.pearl: Prolactin rise can occur with some AAS — DA agonists lower prolactin.

agents.name: Thyroxine (T4)
agents.where: Nuclear thyroid receptors after 5'-deiodinase → T3
agents.pedNote: Exogenous levothyroxine abused for weight loss / metabolic rate.
agents.pearl: T4 is prohormone; T3 (liothyronine) is active — shorter half-life, more potent.

agents.name: Triiodothyronine (T3)
agents.where: Thyroid hormone receptor — basal metabolic rate, β-adrenergic sensitivity
agents.pathway: Peripheral conversion of T4 or direct secretion
agents.pedNote: Liothyronine cuts — catabolic if overdosed; cardiac arrhythmia risk.
agents.pearl: ↑ O₂ consumption, ↑ HR; opposite of hypothyroid myxedema picture.

agents.name: Amphetamine / methylphenidate
agents.where: ↑ catecholamines in synapse — DAT/NET inhibition + release
agents.pedNote: Stimulant class in anti-doping; focus, aggression, decreased perception of fatigue.
agents.pearl: Mechanism distinct from receptor agonists — ↑ synaptic dopamine/NE.

agents.name: Clenbuterol
agents.where: β2 receptors — bronchodilation, skeletal muscle anabolic signal (debated)
agents.pedNote: Veterinary bronchodilator abused for cutting; tremor, tachycardia, hypokalemia.
agents.pearl: Selective β2 in theory; tachyphylaxis; detectable longer than endogenous catecholamines.

agents.name: Ephedrine / pseudoephedrine
agents.where: ↑ NE release + weak direct agonism
agents.pedNote: Banned/threshold in sport; CV stroke risk.
agents.pearl: MAOI interaction can cause hypertensive crisis — clinical pearl.

agents.name: Caffeine
agents.where: Adenosine receptor antagonism — mild CNS/muscle effect
agents.pathway: WADA once monitored, now removed from prohibited list (high doses still ergogenic)
agents.pedNote: Legal ergogenic at moderate doses; know threshold history for exams.
agents.pearl: Mechanism ≠ catecholamines — adenosine blockade reduces perceived effort.

studyPath.title: Steroid hormones
studyPath.subtitle: Nuclear receptors · HPG & adrenal arms · AAS

studyPath.title: Peptide hormones
studyPath.subtitle: Membrane receptors · GH, EPO, insulin, hCG

studyPath.title: Clinical & detection
studyPath.subtitle: Labs, risks, what you see in practice

items.name: Testosterone esters
items.risk: HPG suppression, polycythemia, dyslipidemia, acne, aggression

labs.high: TSH high in primary hypothyroidism; thyroxine misuse → high free T4/T3 with suppressed TSH

risks: Cardiovascular: MI, stroke, cardiomyopathy, hypertension, dyslipidemia
Hepatic: cholestasis, peliosis, tumors with 17α-alkylated orals
Endocrine: infertility, gynecomastia, adrenal/gonadal axis suppression
Psychiatric: irritability, mania, depression on withdrawal
Renal / metabolic: rhabdomyolysis, hyperviscosity, severe hypoglycemia (insulin)

pathways.title: HPG axis (gonadal)
pathways.subtitle: GnRH pulse → sex steroids
pathways.orientation: The hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis converts pulsatile GnRH into LH/FSH, then into testosterone and its metabolites. This is the axis exogenous androgens shut down.

steps.label: Testosterone
steps.hint: Leydig cell steroid — negative feedback on HPG

forkQuiz.after: Pulsatile GnRH is required — continuous exposure suppresses LH/FSH.
LH → ↑ testosterone synthesis in Leydig cells.
HPG shutdown → testicular atrophy, infertility until recovery.

pathways.title: GH–IGF axis
pathways.subtitle: GHRH / somatostatin → GH → IGF-1
pathways.orientation: Growth hormone is pulsatile from the anterior pituitary; IGF-1 from the liver is the stable downstream signal. rhGH abuse targets this loop.

forkQuiz.after: Posterior pituitary stores ADH/oxytocin — not GH.

forkQuiz.prompt: The best stable blood marker of excess GH is…
forkQuiz.after: GH is pulsatile; IGF-1 integrates exposure.
Octreotide mimics somatostatin — used in acromegaly.

pathways.title: HPA axis
pathways.subtitle: CRH → ACTH → cortisol
pathways.orientation: Stress and inflammation drive CRH → ACTH → cortisol. Exogenous glucocorticoids suppress the entire axis — relevant to masking and adrenal suppression.

forkQuiz.after: CRH from hypothalamus stimulates ACTH release.
Risk of adrenal insufficiency if stopped abruptly after chronic use.

pathways.title: Thyroid axis
pathways.subtitle: TRH → TSH → T4 → T3
pathways.orientation: Thyroid hormone sets metabolic rate. T3 abuse mimics hyperthyroidism — weight loss at the cost of cardiac and muscle risk.

forkQuiz.prompt: T3 is more biologically active because…
forkQuiz.after: T4 → T3 conversion via 5'-deiodinase in periphery.

pathways.title: Erythropoiesis
pathways.subtitle: Hypoxia → EPO → RBC mass
pathways.orientation: Kidney EPO is the hormone arm of oxygen delivery. rhEPO and blood doping raise hematocrit — endurance benefit with thrombosis risk.

forkQuiz.prompt: EPO is produced mainly in the…
forkQuiz.after: Small fraction from liver; renal failure → anemia.

forkQuiz.prompt: EPO acts on erythroid progenitors via…
forkQuiz.after: JAK2 pathway — overlap with polycythemia vera mutations.

pathways.title: Steroid synthesis (overview)
pathways.subtitle: Cholesterol → androgens / cortisol / aldosterone
pathways.orientation: All steroid hormones share cholesterol-derived scaffolding. Knowing the branches explains why enzymes like aromatase and 5α-reductase matter on cycles.

forkQuiz.prompt: The rate-limiting step converting cholesterol to pregnenolone uses…
forkQuiz.after: Also called desmolase — mitochondrial inner membrane.
Adipose and gonadal tissue — relevant to gynecomastia on AAS.
Angiotensin II and K⁺ regulate aldosterone — RAAS.
